prd_meta.title: Weekly US Market Recap PRD (Phased, Isolated Implementation)
prd_meta.description: Build a trust-first weekly US market recap pipeline (search -> fetch -> grounded LLM -> validation -> persistence -> API) with strict source auditability, deterministic citation mapping, and explicit phase-level autonomous validation so implementation agents can verify success independently and report outcomes.
source_documents: chat decisions on source trust, validation policy, API contract, and operational semantics

related_documents.usage_note: If implementation details are ambiguous, consult the design plan first, then update this PRD with any clarifications and phase learnings.

prd_meta.sorting_rule: phases ordered by technical dependency chain; each phase should be independently mergeable and verifiable

core_non_negotiables.grounding: LLM must consume only fetched corpus passed by backend; no online model mode
core_non_negotiables.citation_contract: LLM output references source_indices only; backend resolves to canonical source metadata
core_non_negotiables.trust_fail_policy: Hard validation failures after retries must skip DB insert entirely
core_non_negotiables.idempotency: ON CONFLICT (market, cadence, period_start) DO NOTHING in default mode
core_non_negotiables.source_identity: deterministic source_id derived from canonical URL hash
core_non_negotiables.public_citations: Public API exposes user-facing citations only (bullet citations + deduped sources)
core_non_negotiables.raw_sources_visibility: Internal/debug storage only; not part of public API

agent_validation_protocol.purpose: Allow an implementation agent to prove phase success without human hand-holding.

phase_transition_policy.rule: Agent must stop after each phase and wait for explicit user approval before starting the next phase, even when all gates pass.

learning_feedback_policy.rule: After each phase, agent must write validation summary and implementation learnings back into this same PRD file under that phase.
learning_feedback_policy.next_phase_requirement: Before starting a phase, agent must explicitly review and incorporate previous phase learnings and next_phase_considerations.

requirements_for_every_phase: Run phase-specific automated tests and mandatory project baseline checks relevant to touched code.
Collect objective evidence artifacts (command outputs, API samples, DB assertions, structured logs).
Evaluate pass/fail against phase gates; do not mark complete if any mandatory gate fails.
Produce a concise end-of-phase report with: implemented changes, commands run, evidence, gate results, residual risks, and rollback note.
Update this PRD phase entry with validation_summary, learnings, and next_phase_considerations before requesting user approval.

report_persistence_policy.rule: No separate report artifact file is required. Updating this PRD with phase validation and learnings is sufficient.

baseline_check_policy.rule: Baseline checks are mandatory hard gates. healthcheck and ruff must pass to complete a phase; no pass-with-exception path.

phases.title: Data contract and storage foundation
phases.objective: Establish persistent schema and canonical source data shape that all downstream phases depend on.
scope_includes: Create MarketRecap model and Alembic migration.
Include columns: market, cadence, period_start, period_end, summary, bullets, sources, raw_sources, model, created_at.
Add unique constraint on (market, cadence, period_start).
Add index for latest queries: (market, period_start DESC).
Define source object schema with deterministic source_id.
Codify canonical URL utility and source_id generation utility (hash of canonical URL).
scope_excludes: No search/fetch/LLM orchestration yet.
No API endpoint yet.
No scheduler/deployment.

implementation_checks: Migration applies cleanly up and down in local dev DB.
ORM model matches migration columns and constraints.
Canonical URL + source_id utility has deterministic behavior.
JSON schema contracts for bullets and citations are explicit and reusable by validator/API layers.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run migration up/down cycle in local environment.
Run model/schema unit tests for serialization and source_id determinism.
Run baseline checks for touched backend files.
required_evidence: passing schema/unit tests
migration success log (upgrade + downgrade + upgrade)
example canonicalization input/output fixture
example source_id stability fixture
phase_gates: Gate A: migration integrity pass
Gate B: schema determinism pass
Gate C: lint/health baseline pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 1 data contract and storage foundation: canonical URL/source_id utilities, reusable Pydantic recap payload schemas, MarketRecap ORM model, Alembic migration, and focused unit/DB coverage. Phase-specific tests, healthcheck, ruff, and the market_recap migration up/down/up round trip passed against stonkie_test.
commands_executed: DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/stonkie_test alembic stamp d4e5f6a7b8c9 && alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head

learnings: source_id is the first 16 chars of sha256(canonical_url).hexdigest(); canonicalization normalizes scheme/host/default ports/trailing slash and strips utm_*, fbclid, gclid, mc_cid, mc_eid, ref, and ref_src.
RecapPayload enforces citation integrity at schema level: every bullet must have at least one citation and every citation.source_id must exist in sources[].id.
MarketRecap stores public payload pieces in bullets/sources JSON columns and keeps raw_sources as internal free-form JSON.
A historical root migration attempted to drop company_financials objects that may not exist on a fresh DB; it was made idempotent with DROP IF EXISTS so fresh test DB migration can proceed further.

Local blank-DB replay still requires the pgvector Postgres extension for the existing semantic_cache migration. Phase 1 tests therefore stamp stonkie_test at the previous revision and validate the new market_recap migration up/down/up from there.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: migration integrity pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: market_recap migration upgraded from d4e5f6a7b8c9 to e5f6a7b8c9d0, downgraded -1, then upgraded again on stonkie_test. Full blank-chain replay remains environment-dependent on pgvector.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: schema determinism pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: URL/source_id, schema round-trip, citation min_length, and cross-reference tests passed.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: lint/health baseline pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py passed and ruff check . passed.

next_phase_considerations: Phase 2 retrieval should use canonicalize_url/source_id_for for dedupe and stable source identity before candidate ranking.
Generator/validator phases should consume RecapPayload rather than redefining citation/source contracts.
Install pgvector in local Postgres or use an already-migrated dev DB when validating the entire historical Alembic chain from an empty database.

phases.title: Retrieval pipeline and source-policy controls
phases.objective: Implement query planning, Tavily retrieval/normalization, and candidate ranking under soft allowlist policy.

rejected: Google CSE (closed to new customers; sunset 2027-01-01)
Perplexity Sonar (pre-synthesizes answers; violates grounding contract)
provider_decision.rationale: Tavily collapses search + content extraction into one call, has native date/domain/news filters that map to our query planner and source-policy layers, and keeps Phase 2 implementation small with a single provider.

scope_includes: Implement query planner with open queries + selected site-scoped queries.
Implement Tavily search client and response normalization (single provider; client interface designed to allow future providers without touching ranking/source-policy).
Use Tavily-extracted content as the sole Phase 2 content source; no fallback fetcher in this phase.
Implement source allowlist in code and registrable-domain matching (consider passing as Tavily include_domains as an optimization, but enforcement stays in code).
Use Tavily start_date/end_date as the authoritative publish-date filter for Phase 2 candidate eligibility.
Implement canonical dedupe and ranking priorities (freshness, allowlist preference, diversity, relevance).
Store/carry raw candidate metadata for internal debugging.
scope_excludes: No LLM generation yet.
No persistence insert decisions yet.
No public API changes yet.

implementation_checks: Query planner emits both open and site-scoped query sets.
Tavily client returns normalized candidate objects (title, url, snippet, published_date, extracted_content) and is mockable in tests with no live calls.
Search-client interface is provider-shaped so a future fallback (e.g. Brave) can be added without touching ranking or source-policy code.
Source-policy correctly identifies allowlisted domains via normalized registrable domains.
Candidates with missing/empty Tavily extracted_content are excluded before ranking.
Tavily start_date/end_date filtering is treated as authoritative for out-of-week exclusion in Phase 2.
Ranking and dedupe are deterministic for identical input sets.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run unit tests with mocked search/fetch responses.
Run one controlled dry-run fixture through retrieval pipeline and capture candidate counts per stage.
Confirm allowlist classification and Tavily-date-filter assumptions with explicit fixtures.
required_evidence: Tavily response fixture normalized into candidate objects with required fields
fixture showing allowlisted and non-allowlisted domain classification
fixture showing candidates with empty extracted_content are excluded before ranking
stage counter snapshot: results_total -> deduped -> with_raw_content -> allowlisted -> ranked_top_k
deterministic dedupe/ranking output for stable test input
phase_gates: Gate A: retrieval policy tests pass
Gate B: date/source policy tests pass
Gate C: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 2 retrieval and source-policy layer with strict TDD slices: allowlist classification, query planning, provider-shaped Tavily client normalization, deterministic dedupe/ranking, and retrieval coordinator with stage counters and top-k output. Added manual smoke script integration to run the new Tavily client against live API responses and verified normalization behavior end-to-end.

learnings: Tavily published_date can arrive as either ISO8601 or RFC2822; normalization now accepts both formats and degrades to null for unparsable values.
Using a provider Protocol (SearchProvider) keeps retrieval/ranking layers decoupled from Tavily-specific transport details.
source_id_for + canonicalize_url provides stable dedupe semantics across tracking-parameter URL variants.
with_raw_content filtering before ranking aligns ranking quality with usable citation corpus and matches stage-counter expectations.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: retrieval policy tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: query planner, tavily normalization, ranking/dedupe, and retrieval stats/top-k tests passed.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: date/source policy tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: allowlist registrable-domain classification and Tavily date-format normalization assumptions were validated by fixtures/tests.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py passed and ruff check . passed.

next_phase_considerations: Phase 3 generator should consume RetrievalResult candidates directly and preserve source_id mapping for citation integrity.
Keep model-output citation contract source_indices-only; do not allow free-form URL text paths.
If Tavily date quality drifts in production, add explicit anomaly logging before reconsidering secondary date filtering.

phases.title: Grounded recap generation and trust-critical validation
phases.objective: Implement LLM generation contract and hard validator so only trustworthy recaps are eligible for persistence.
scope_includes: Implement recap generator with existing MultiAgent (offline context only; no online mode).
Prompt design enforcing source_indices-only references and neutral tone best effort.
Resolve source_indices into canonical source objects with source_id mapping.
Implement validator hard checks: schema, source existence, date in window, per-bullet min sources, and allowlist expectation logic; recap-wide unique source floor is warning-only (non-blocking).
Implement split policy: style/length/tone are prompt-only and not hard-fail conditions.
scope_excludes: No DB insert orchestration retries yet.
No scheduler/task wiring yet.

implementation_checks: Generator accepts only provided corpus and emits strict JSON parseable payload.
No free-form URL acceptance from model output path.
Validator rejects unknown/hallucinated source references.
Validator rejects unparseable or out-of-window cited dates.
Validator enforces trust rules while allowing style constraints to remain non-blocking.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run unit tests for positive and negative validator paths.
Run mocked generator contract tests to ensure source_indices mapping works.
Prove at least one intentionally malformed payload is rejected for each hard rule class.
required_evidence: contract test proving free-form URL output is ignored/rejected
validator test proving unknown URL reference fails
validator test proving out-of-window date fails
validator test proving minimum source-count rules are enforced
validator test proving recap-wide unique source floor emits warning-only signal (non-blocking)
phase_gates: Gate A: generator contract tests pass
Gate B: trust-validator negative/positive matrix pass
Gate C: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 3 grounded generation and trust-validator modules via strict test-first slices. Generator now builds deterministic indexed-corpus prompts, enforces offline model invocation, parses fenced JSON payloads, maps source_indices to canonical source objects/source_ids, and returns audit model metadata. Validator enforces out-of-window and per-bullet allowlist hard failures while treating recap-wide unique-source floor as warning-only.

learnings: Model stream chunks can include non-string citation annotations; generator must ignore dict chunks before marker extraction/JSON parsing.
Keeping source_indices as the only model citation primitive allows backend-owned deterministic source_id mapping and URL canonicalization.
Recap-wide unique source floor can be monitored as an operational quality warning without blocking valid recaps that satisfy hard trust rules.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: generator contract tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Prompt composition, marker parsing, source-index mapping, dedupe behavior, dict-chunk filtering, and model-audit field tests passed.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: trust-validator negative/positive matrix pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Out-of-window failure, per-bullet allowlist failure, grace-day handling, failure accumulation, and unique-source warning-only behavior tests passed.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py passed and ruff check . passed.

next_phase_considerations: Phase 4 should wire GeneratorError and ValidationResult failures into retry policy and skip-insert semantics without mutating phase-3 trust rules.
Keep persistence-layer model audit column populated from GeneratorResult.model for run-level traceability.
Preserve warning-only handling for unique-source floor in orchestration logs/metrics while keeping hard failures reserved for trust invariants.

phases.title: End-to-end run orchestration, persistence, retries, and backfill controls
phases.objective: Wire complete weekly recap run path with idempotent persistence semantics and operator-safe execution modes.
scope_includes: Implement orchestration service combining retrieval, generation, validation, and persistence.
Add run-level retry behavior in orchestrator/CLI flow (no Celery dependency in v1).
Persist success rows with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING default behavior.
Skip insert on hard validation failure after retries; structured error logging required.
Add CLI runner with default latest completed week mode (America/New_York).
Add backfill mode with explicit range and safety span guard.
Add guarded --replace mode for exact period correction workflow.
scope_excludes: No public API endpoint yet.
No Cloud Run scheduler deployment yet.

implementation_checks: Successful run inserts one recap row for target period.
Repeat run same period does not overwrite row in default mode.
Failure run does not insert partial/degraded row.
CLI default computes latest completed week correctly in US market timezone.
Backfill and replace flags behave safely and predictably.

agent_self_validation_steps: Execute local run for fixed historical week and assert DB insert.
Re-run same week and assert idempotent no-op insert.
Force validation failure fixture and assert insert skip + error log emission.
Execute backfill one-week and explicit range commands.
Execute replace flow on exact period in controlled local fixture.
required_commands: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market US --period-start 2026-04-20 --period-end 2026-04-24
required_evidence: DB query evidence: inserted row exists after success run
DB query evidence: row count unchanged on rerun default mode
log evidence: validation fail path emits structured error and skips insert
CLI help/usage evidence for default, backfill range, and replace modes
phase_gates: Gate A: orchestration E2E pass
Gate B: idempotency and failure semantics pass
Gate C: CLI operational safety pass
Gate D: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 4 with strict TDD slices: persistence module with idempotent default insert and guarded replace mode, orchestration module wiring retrieval/generation/validation/persistence with retry-on-failure semantics, and argparse CLI runner with latest completed US week default plus backfill/replace safety checks.
commands_executed: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market US --period-start 2026-04-20 --period-end 2026-04-24

source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market US --period-start 2026-04-20 --period-end 2026-04-24 && source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python -c "from connectors.database import SessionLocal; from models.market_recap import MarketRecap; from datetime import date; from sqlalchemy import select, func; with SessionLocal() as db: c=db.execute(select(func.count()).select_from(MarketRecap).where(MarketRecap.market=='US', MarketRecap.cadence=='weekly', MarketRecap.period_start==date(2026,4,20))).scalar_one(); print(c)"

source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python -c "from datetime import date, datetime, UTC; from sqlalchemy import select, func; from connectors.database import SessionLocal; from models.market_recap import MarketRecap; from services.market_recap.orchestrator import run_market_recap; from services.market_recap.recap_generator import GeneratorResult; from services.market_recap.schemas import RetrievalResult, RetrievalStats, Candidate, RecapPayload, Bullet, Citation, Source; from services.market_recap.validator import ValidationResult, REASON_OUT_OF_WINDOW; retrieval=RetrievalResult(candidates=[Candidate(title='x',url='https://www.reuters.com/x',snippet='s',published_date=datetime(2026,5,1,12,0,tzinfo=UTC),raw_content='body',score=1.0,provider='tavily')],stats=RetrievalStats(queries_total=1,results_total=1,deduped=1,with_raw_content=1,allowlisted=1,ranked_top_k=1)); payload=RecapPayload(period_start=date(2026,4,27),period_end=date(2026,5,1),summary='s',bullets=[Bullet(text='b',citations=[Citation(source_id='src')])],sources=[Source(id='src',url='https://www.reuters.com/x',title='t',publisher='reuters.com',published_at=datetime(2026,5,1,12,0,tzinfo=UTC),fetched_at=datetime(2026,5,1,12,0,tzinfo=UTC))]); res=run_market_recap(market='US',cadence='weekly',period_start=date(2026,4,27),period_end=date(2026,5,1),max_attempts=2,retrieve_fn=lambda **_: retrieval,generate_fn=lambda **_: GeneratorResult(payload=payload,model='m',raw_model_output='r'),validate_fn=lambda **_: ValidationResult(ok=False,failures=[REASON_OUT_OF_WINDOW],warnings=[])); print(res.status,res.attempts,res.validation_failures); with SessionLocal() as db: c=db.execute(select(func.count()).select_from(MarketRecap).where(MarketRecap.market=='US',MarketRecap.cadence=='weekly',MarketRecap.period_start==date(2026,4,27))).scalar_one(); print(c)"

learnings: Keeping persistence in a small module (`persist_recap`) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + optional replace made idempotency behavior explicit and easy to verify at DB level.
Injectable orchestrator dependencies enabled deterministic retry/failure-path tests without live Tavily/LLM calls while preserving a default production path.
CLI safety guardrails (`--replace` requiring exact period and bounded backfill weeks) prevent accidental destructive operations.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: orchestration E2E pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Orchestrator success path inserts recap row; validation failure and generation failure paths retry and skip insert as required.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: idempotency and failure semantics pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Default rerun for same period preserved row count at 1; forced validation-failure run produced validation_failed status and zero inserts for target period.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: CLI operational safety pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: CLI tests validated latest completed week computation, explicit period execution, max-span backfill rejection, and replace flag constraints.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate D: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py passed and ruff check . passed.

next_phase_considerations: Phase 5 should read persisted recap rows only; keep `raw_sources` internal and excluded from API response contracts.
API layer should expose deterministic source_id join compatibility between bullets citations and top-level sources as persisted in Phase 4 payload shape.
When adding observability in Phase 6, emit run_id + inserted/skipped status from orchestrator outcomes without altering retry semantics.

phases.title: Public API contract and citation mapping usability
phases.objective: Expose recap data via stable API envelope with explicit freshness metadata and deterministic citation mapping fields.
scope_includes: Implement markets router endpoint for recaps retrieval.
Return metadata + items envelope, with latest_created_at convenience field.
Per item include period_start, period_end, created_at.
Expose bullets and top-level deduped sources with shared source_id mapping compatibility.
Return latest available rows when latest week is missing.
Keep raw_sources out of public response.
scope_excludes: No frontend implementation required in this backend PRD.
No scheduler/deploy concerns.

implementation_checks: Endpoint returns envelope with metadata and items.
Freshness fields are present and correct.
Citation mapping via source_id is deterministic and frontend-friendly.
raw_sources is not exposed in API response.
Limit/order behavior returns latest periods first.

agent_self_validation_steps: Seed at least two recap rows and query endpoint with varying limits.
Validate response schema and field presence.
Validate source_id mapping consistency between bullets and top-level sources.
Validate behavior when a recent period is absent (returns latest available silently).
required_evidence: sample API JSON response with metadata + items
assertions proving source_id joins bullets->sources
assertion proving raw_sources absent from public payload
assertion proving descending period ordering
phase_gates: Gate A: API contract test pass
Gate B: citation mapping test pass
Gate C: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 5 public recap API with strict TDD slices: new markets router endpoint, DB-backed recap query path, stable metadata+items envelope, and deterministic citation/source_id compatibility for frontend rendering. Endpoint now returns latest available persisted rows with order/limit controls and keeps raw_sources internal-only.

learnings: Injecting DB session via FastAPI dependency (`get_db`) in the markets endpoint is essential for deterministic API integration tests; direct SessionLocal usage makes test seeding and endpoint reads diverge.
Normalizing datetime output to UTC Z-format in the API layer avoids timezone-offset drift in client contracts and keeps latest_created_at deterministic.
Phase 5 behavior requirements (ordering, limit, latest_available, raw_sources exclusion) can be fully specified as public HTTP tests without mocking market_recap internals.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: API contract test pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/api/test_markets_recaps.py passed with 7/7 green, covering envelope fields, empty-state response, latest-available behavior, and order/limit semantics.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: citation mapping test pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Tracer-bullet and full API tests assert bullets[].citations[].source_id values map to sources[].id in response payload.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py passed and ruff check . passed (including import-order fix in main.py).

next_phase_considerations: Phase 6 observability should emit structured API read metrics (market, cadence, limit, item_count) without leaking raw_sources.
If frontend needs stricter response typing, introduce explicit response models while preserving current field names and source_id join semantics.
Keep timezone normalization in one shared serializer helper if additional recap endpoints are introduced.

phases.title: Structured observability and operational diagnostics
phases.objective: Ensure every run is diagnosable via structured logs and quality counters before enabling unattended schedule.
scope_includes: Emit structured run-level counters and dimensions.

Include required fields: run_id, market, cadence, period_start, period_end, queries_total, results_total, fetched_ok, date_in_window_count, allowlisted_count, cited_count, validation_fail_reason, inserted.
Ensure logs capture both success and failure paths clearly.
Document log interpretation guide for operators/agents.
scope_excludes: No alert integration (logs-only v1).
No additional monitoring platform integration required.

implementation_checks: Structured log events emitted for run start, run outcome, and key stage transitions.
Failure logs include explicit reason code and context.
Success logs include enough counters to support quality tuning without rerun.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run one successful local recap and capture structured log object.
Run one forced-failure local recap and capture structured failure log object.
Verify required fields and value types are present.
Cross-check cited_count and inserted flags align with DB reality.
required_evidence: success log JSON sample
failure log JSON sample
field-level checklist proving required counters exist
DB/log consistency note for inserted and cited_count
phase_gates: Gate A: observability tests pass
Gate B: success/failure log completeness pass
Gate C: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 6 structured observability via TDD: added services/market_recap/logging.py (new_run_id + log_event helpers using stdlib logging extra), wired recap.run.start and recap.run.outcome events into orchestrator with run_id correlation across all four return sites (success, generation_failed early-exit, validation_failed terminal, generation_failed exhausted-loop), and authored docs/market_recap_operations.md operator guide. All required PRD outcome fields present including run_id, market, cadence, period_start, period_end, queries_total, results_total, fetched_ok, date_in_window_count, allowlisted_count, cited_count, validation_fail_reason, inserted, status. RunResult shape unchanged (run_id intentionally internal to logs).
commands_executed: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/services/market_recap/ tests/scripts/ tests/api/ -v

learnings: alembic/env.py was calling logging.config.fileConfig without disable_existing_loggers=False, which silently disabled the orchestrator's module-level logger after the test session-scoped alembic upgrade ran. Changed env.py to pass disable_existing_loggers=False — preserves alembic/sqlalchemy logger config from alembic.ini while keeping app loggers alive. Without this fix caplog captured zero records from any orchestrator-emitted event.

Naming the new helper services/market_recap/logging.py is safe in Python 3 because absolute imports route bare `import logging` to stdlib; we still import the helper by explicit symbols (`from services.market_recap.logging import log_event, new_run_id`) to avoid shadowing.

Tavily start_date/end_date is the authoritative phase-2 date filter, so `date_in_window_count` is currently equal to `results_total`; documented in operations doc so future phases can diverge them only if a post-Tavily date re-check is added.
Using stdlib `logging.info(event, extra={...})` keeps the implementation dependency-free and lets Cloud Logging surface `extra` as `jsonPayload` without a custom formatter.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: observability tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/services/market_recap/test_observability.py 4/4 passed: success start+outcome with all required fields, validation-failure logs reason and skip, generation-failure logs zero cited and skip, run_id stable hex correlation across events.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: success/failure log completeness pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Test asserts REQUIRED_FIELDS set (run_id, market, cadence, period_start, period_end, queries_total, results_total, fetched_ok, date_in_window_count, allowlisted_count, cited_count, validation_fail_reason, inserted) is a subset of outcome event fields and that fields dict is JSON-serializable. inserted=true correlates with persisted DB row in success test.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: tests/test_healthcheck.py 3/3 passed; ruff check . clean. Full regression sweep across tests/services/market_recap/ tests/scripts/ tests/api/ = 67 passed.

next_phase_considerations: Before starting Phase 7, read backend/docs/market_recap_operations.md end-to-end — it is the canonical reference for event names, field semantics, status values, log-based diagnostics, and the rollback/rerun procedure that the production dry-run must validate.
Phase 7 deploy/scheduler should rely on these structured events for dry-run validation; verify Cloud Run picks up `extra` as jsonPayload in shipped logs (no custom formatter needed in stdlib path).

Phase 7 must extend backend/docs/market_recap_operations.md with the deploy/runbook section (Cloud Run job name, scheduler enable/disable, dry-run procedure, rollback drill) rather than creating a parallel doc.
If a future phase introduces post-Tavily date re-validation, split `date_in_window_count` from `results_total` and update backend/docs/market_recap_operations.md accordingly.
When adding alerting (deferred per PRD), prefer log-based metrics keyed off `event=recap.run.outcome AND inserted=false` rather than introducing a new emission path.

phases.title: Brave LLM-Context provider for VN market and daily cadence support
phases.objective: Route VN market recap retrieval through Brave LLM-Context (replacing Tavily for VN due to poor Vietnamese-language coverage) and add daily cadence as a manually-runnable execution mode, prioritizing source quality over throughput.

rationale.evidence_summary: Side-by-side test on prior business week (2026-04-20..04-24) with query 'thị trường chứng khoán Việt Nam tuần qua' showed Tavily returns 0 Vietnamese-language sources even unconstrained (5/5 hits were ft/tipranks/aastocks/sfchronicle/reuters); constraining Tavily to the VN allowlist forced all 5 hits onto reuters.com with only 1/5 actually about Vietnam. Brave returned 8 hits with 3/8 on the original VN allowlist and 5/8 on legitimate VN financial press not yet allowlisted. Sweep across country/search_lang/count combos confirmed country=ALL, search_lang=vi, count=30 maximizes allowlist hit rate; count beyond 20 does not expand recall but is harmless. With expanded allowlist, allowlist hit rate rises to 71-86%.

provider_strategy.rule: Brave fully replaces Tavily for VN. US continues on Tavily. No feature flag, no fallback path; VN-Tavily code path deleted.
provider_strategy.routing: SearchProvider selection in retrieval.py keyed by market: US -> TavilyClient, VN -> BraveClient.

cadence_extension.daily_window: period_start == period_end == latest completed trading day in Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh.
cadence_extension.scheduler: deferred. Phase 6.5 only adds CLI/orchestrator support for cadence=daily; Cloud Scheduler/Cloud Run wiring lives in Phase 7 or later.
cadence_extension.weekend_holiday_handling: CLI default skips Saturdays and Sundays for VN daily; explicit --period-start can override. TET/holiday calendar not modeled in v1.

vn_allowlist_expansion.tiering: single-tier in v1 (no tier-1/tier-2 split); revisit if quality drift observed.
vn_allowlist_expansion.rationale: Test runs surfaced legitimate VN financial coverage from sources missing from the original 11-entry list. Expansion increases yield while staying within editorially-vetted outlets (state press, regulators, exchanges, top-4 brokers, mainstream business sections).

registrable_domain_fix.approach: Hardcode known multi-part VN suffixes in services/market_recap/source_policy.py: .com.vn, .gov.vn, .org.vn, .net.vn, .edu.vn. Strip the suffix and take the last remaining label + suffix.
registrable_domain_fix.rejected_alternative: tldextract dependency (rejected for v1: avoids new transitive dep).
registrable_domain_fix.test_coverage: must include cases for ssi.com.vn, hsx.vn, ssc.gov.vn, hsc.com.vn, vir.com.vn (new); reuters.com, cafef.vn (existing 2-part); www.cafef.vn subdomain.
registrable_domain_fix.future_consideration: If allowlist expands to .co.uk / other 2-label public suffixes, switch to tldextract.

brave_request_shape.freshness: YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD (authoritative window filter; same posture as Tavily start_date/end_date)
brave_request_shape.goggles: inline Goggle definition passed per request that boosts allowlisted VN domains. No external Goggle hosting in v1.
brave_request_shape.context_threshold_mode: balanced (Brave default; revisit to strict if low-quality results observed)
brave_request_shape.country_sweep_evidence: ALL/vi=71-86%, ALL/en=71%, HK/vi=71%, US/vi=71%, JP/vi=57% allowlisted. ALL/vi chosen.

query_plan.strategy: single broad query per run (no multi-query merge in v1); planner emits one PlannedQuery with empty include_domains.
query_plan.weekly_template_vn: thị trường chứng khoán Việt Nam tuần qua
query_plan.daily_template_vn: thị trường chứng khoán Việt Nam phiên hôm nay
query_plan.rationale: Brave returned a stable 7-hit ceiling regardless of count; multi-query was deferred per cost/complexity trade-off.

snippet_handling.policy: concatenate Brave grounding.generic[].snippets[] verbatim into Candidate.raw_content. No JSON-LD parsing, no separate re-fetcher.
snippet_handling.rationale: Snippets sometimes embed raw NewsArticle JSON-LD strings; LLM can still read the embedded articleBody. Avoids new HTML/JSON parser surface area in v1.
snippet_handling.stage_filter: existing with_raw_content filter still applies (drop candidates with empty concatenated raw_content).

published_date_policy.primary: iterate Brave sources[<url>].age list and use first parseable value (ISO 'YYYY-MM-DD' typically present at index 1).
published_date_policy.fallback: (period_start + period_end) / 2 at 12:00 UTC when no element of the age list parses.
published_date_policy.out_of_window_check: trust Brave freshness param as authoritative; do not post-filter dates. date_in_window_count == results_total documented assumption (mirrors Tavily).
published_date_policy.drop_policy: do NOT drop candidates with unparseable dates; midpoint fallback keeps yield.

validator_vn_rule_split.per_bullet_allowlist: warning-only for VN (was hard-fail); reason: with looser allowlist enforcement at the provider layer, occasional non-allowlisted citations should not block the recap.
validator_vn_rule_split.recap_wide_floor: hard-fail when recap has fewer than 2 distinct allowlisted sources across all bullets (NEW). Replaces the warning-only floor for VN.
validator_vn_rule_split.us_unchanged: US validator rules untouched; rule split lives behind a market parameter on the validator entrypoint.

retrieval_floor.rationale: Validator's recap-wide >=2 allowlisted sources rule already blocks thin recaps post-generation; pre-LLM cutoff would risk false skips on quieter VN news days. LLM-token cost accepted.

provider_persistence.decision: do not add a `provider` column/field to the Source schema in v1.
provider_persistence.rationale: Per-source provider tracking can be reconstructed from logs (run_id -> provider) if needed. Defer schema change until a concrete analysis use-case exists.
provider_persistence.observability_carry: orchestrator structured log gains a `provider` field on the run.start and run.outcome events (free-form string).

scope_includes: Implement BraveClient (services/market_recap/brave_client.py) conforming to existing SearchProvider Protocol; normalize grounding.generic[] -> Candidate with concatenated snippets as raw_content and Brave-window-midpoint fallback for unparseable published_date.
Add inline Goggle definition built from the VN allowlist; pass via Brave goggles parameter on every VN request.
Expand ALLOWLIST_BY_MARKET['VN'] in source_policy.py with the 19 additions listed in design_decisions.vn_allowlist_expansion.additions.
Fix registrable_domain in source_policy.py to handle multi-part VN suffixes (.com.vn / .gov.vn / .org.vn / .net.vn / .edu.vn).
Add provider routing in retrieval.py keyed by market (US -> Tavily, VN -> Brave).
Delete the VN-Tavily code path; remove TAVILY default for VN.
Extend query_planner.py with VN query templates per cadence (weekly: 'thị trường chứng khoán Việt Nam tuần qua'; daily: 'thị trường chứng khoán Việt Nam phiên hôm nay').

Extend orchestrator.py to accept cadence='daily', dispatch to provider per market, default daily window to latest completed trading day in Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh, and emit `provider` and `cadence` in run.start/run.outcome structured logs.
Extend validator.py with a market-aware rule split: per-bullet allowlist warning-only for VN, recap-wide >=2 distinct allowlisted sources hard-fail for VN. US rules unchanged.
Extend run_market_recap.py CLI with --cadence weekly|daily and VN-aware tz default (Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh).
Add BRAVE_API_KEY to .env.example.
Update backend/docs/market_recap_operations.md with Brave request shape, expanded VN allowlist, registrable_domain VN suffix policy, daily cadence runbook section, and the VN validator rule split.
scope_excludes: No Cloud Scheduler / Cloud Run wiring for VN or daily (Phase 7 or later).
No JSON-LD parsing of Brave snippets; raw_content stays verbatim.
No external Goggle hosting; Goggles inline only.
No HTML re-fetcher.
No tldextract dependency.
No `provider` column on the Source schema.
No tier-1/tier-2 split inside the VN allowlist.
No multi-query plan for VN.
No retrieval-floor hard-fail.
No alerting integration.

implementation_checks: BraveClient returns normalized Candidate objects given a fixture Brave response, with raw_content = concatenated snippets and published_date = parsed age | window-midpoint fallback.
Inline Goggle is constructed deterministically from the VN allowlist and included in every VN Brave request.
registrable_domain correctly resolves all VN allowlist entries including multi-part suffixes (ssi.com.vn, hsx.vn, ssc.gov.vn, hsc.com.vn, vir.com.vn).
retrieval.py dispatches market='VN' to BraveClient and market='US' to TavilyClient.
orchestrator accepts cadence='daily' and computes default period as latest completed trading day in Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh.
Validator with market='VN' downgrades per-bullet allowlist to warning and hard-fails when recap-wide distinct-allowlisted-sources < 2.
Validator with market='US' is byte-identical to pre-Phase-6.5 behavior.
CLI --cadence daily runs end-to-end against fixture and inserts a row keyed by (market='VN', cadence='daily', period_start).

agent_self_validation_steps: Run all phase-specific pytest suites with mocked Brave fixture (no live calls).
Run smoke against live Brave for a fixed prior trading day (manual; not part of pytest).
Confirm DB row inserted for (market='VN', cadence='daily', period_start=<chosen day>).
Confirm structured run.outcome log carries provider='brave', cadence='daily', cited_count, allowlisted_count consistent with DB row.
Confirm US weekly path is unchanged via existing test_orchestrator and test_validator suites.
Confirm VN-Tavily code path is fully removed (grep).
required_commands: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market VN --cadence weekly --period-start 2026-04-20 --period-end 2026-04-24
source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market VN --cadence daily
required_evidence: Brave fixture normalized into Candidate objects with raw_content and parseable/midpoint-fallback dates
registrable_domain test matrix proving correct resolution for .com.vn, .gov.vn, .org.vn, .net.vn, .edu.vn entries
provider routing test proving market='US' uses Tavily and market='VN' uses Brave
validator test matrix: VN per-bullet allowlist downgrade (warning), VN recap-wide >=2 hard-fail, US rules unchanged
DB query evidence: row inserted for (VN, daily, <day>) and (VN, weekly, <week>)
structured run.outcome log JSON sample showing provider='brave' and cadence='daily'/'weekly'
grep evidence: no remaining references to TavilyClient under market='VN' code path
phase_gates: Gate A: Brave client + provider routing tests pass
Gate B: VN allowlist + multi-suffix registrable_domain tests pass
Gate C: validator VN rule split tests pass; US rules unchanged
Gate D: daily cadence orchestrator/CLI tests pass and end-to-end manual run succeeds
Gate E: baseline lint/health pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 6.5 with production Brave integration and cadence expansion. VN retrieval now runs through Brave with market-aware request params, strict threshold mode, inline goggles, expanded VN allowlist, and multi-query planning. Daily cadence support and validator split behavior were validated through unit suites and end-to-end runs. Additional hardening completed for Brave date parsing and pre-ranking out-of-window filtering, followed by multi-week comparison/benchmark runs.
commands_executed: source venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/run_market_recap.py --market VN --period-start 2026-04-20 --period-end 2026-04-24 --replace

learnings: Brave freshness + strict threshold improves precision but is not sufficient alone; local date hardening (age parsing + URL/title date hints + pre-rank out-of-window filtering) is required for stable week-window validation.
VN domain matching required custom multi-part suffix handling (.com.vn/.gov.vn/.org.vn/.net.vn/.edu.vn) to avoid false negatives in allowlist checks.
Multi-query planning materially increases Brave retrieval headroom and makes provider comparison with Tavily fairer.
Source quality risk is mostly ranking-driven; adding page-shape quality scoring significantly reduces hub/index/landing-page leakage into top-k.
With equal query strategy and top_k=10, Brave showed stronger candidate volume and faster runtime while meeting validation gates after hardening.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: Brave client + provider routing tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Brave client normalization, market-specific request params, and retrieval routing tests passed.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: VN allowlist + multi-suffix registrable_domain tests pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Expanded VN allowlist and multi-suffix domain extraction logic validated by unit tests.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: validator VN rule split tests pass; US rules unchanged
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Validator behavior aligns with VN/US rule split expectations; trust-critical checks remain enforced.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate D: daily cadence orchestrator/CLI tests pass and end-to-end manual run succeeds
gates_result_snapshot.notes: VN weekly and daily flows executed successfully; E2E run confirmed by operator and local evidence.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate E: baseline lint/health pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Healthcheck and ruff checks passed.

next_phase_considerations: If migrating US from Tavily to Brave, prefer soft rollout (Brave primary + Tavily fallback) until additional week-level monitoring confirms stability.
Retain ranking quality scoring and continue tuning medium-quality roundup/live sources (e.g. market live pages) only if they materially degrade recap quality.
Document provider-selection policy and rollout criteria in operations docs before enabling unattended scheduler migration behavior.

phases.title: Cloud Run job, scheduler wiring, and production dry-run validation
phases.objective: Ship operational execution path with one controlled dry run before enabling recurring Saturday automation.
scope_includes: Add Dockerfile.market-recap aligned with existing job patterns.
Define Cloud Run job and Cloud Scheduler trigger at Saturday cadence.
Document deployment/runbook steps in existing cronjob docs.
Execute one manual production dry-run and verify inserted row + API visibility + logs.
scope_excludes: No alerting integration beyond logs-only policy.
No multi-market expansion in this phase.

implementation_checks: Cloud Run job deploys and executes successfully.
Scheduler config exists but recurring trigger is enabled only after dry-run signoff.
Manual production dry-run inserts expected recap row and endpoint serves it.
Operational docs include rollback and rerun guidance.

agent_self_validation_steps: Build/deploy job image and run manual trigger in production/staging target.
Confirm job exit status and structured run logs.
Confirm DB row insertion for target period.
Confirm API returns inserted row with expected metadata/citations.
Capture rollback drill note (disable schedule and/or rerun behavior).
required_evidence: Cloud Run execution result snapshot
structured logs from dry-run
DB assertion for inserted recap row
API response sample proving row visibility
documented enable/disable scheduler procedure
phase_gates: Gate A: deploy/run pass
Gate B: data/API verification pass
Gate C: operations runbook completeness pass

validation_summary.summary: Implemented Phase 7 as a deployment-only phase: created Dockerfile.market-recap (python:3.12.5-slim, no Playwright), pushed linux/amd64 image to new Artifact Registry repo market-recap-repo, created Cloud Run job weekly-market-recap (1 CPU / 2 Gi, europe-north1), created Cloud Scheduler weekly-market-recap-scheduler (0 8 * * 6, Europe/Helsinki, europe-west1). Executed two manual dry-runs: first confirmed US + VN rows inserted for 2026-04-20..04-24; second confirmed idempotent no-op (row count stayed at 2). Baseline healthcheck and ruff passed. Created docs/gcp_cronjobs.md and updated docs/market_recap_operations.md with production deployment and rollback sections. Confirmed Tavily is no longer used — only BRAVE_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL are required env vars.
commands_executed: gcloud scheduler jobs create http weekly-market-recap-scheduler --location=europe-west1 --schedule='0 8 * * 6' --time-zone='Europe/Helsinki' ...
DELETE FROM market_recap WHERE cadence='weekly' AND period_start='2026-04-20' (deleted 3 rows)
gcloud run jobs execute weekly-market-recap --region=europe-north1 --wait (execution weekly-market-recap-47j8g: Succeeded)
gcloud run jobs execute weekly-market-recap --region=europe-north1 --wait (execution weekly-market-recap-9bxpm: Succeeded, idempotency rerun)

learnings: docker buildx build --push sometimes completes layer push without applying the manifest tag; follow up with docker buildx imagetools create --tag to explicitly tag if Artifact Registry shows image with no tags.
Both US and VN now use Brave exclusively (Tavily removed from retrieval.py); TAVILY_API_KEY is not required for the Cloud Run job.
Scheduler must be in europe-west1 (Cloud Scheduler does not support europe-north1); Cloud Run job stays in europe-north1.
linux/amd64 cross-build from Apple Silicon requires docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 to match production architecture.

gcloud run jobs update --args splits on commas, so passing --args='python,scripts/foo.py,--markets,US,VN,FI' delivers US, VN, FI as three separate args instead of one comma-separated string. Always bake multi-value args into the Dockerfile CMD (JSON array form) and clear --args override; never rely on --args for comma-separated argument values.
Adding a new market (FI) requires no code changes if source_policy.py and query_planner.py already handle it — just update the --markets list in the Dockerfile CMD and rebuild the image.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate A: deploy/run pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: Cloud Run job created and executed successfully (execution weekly-market-recap-47j8g). Scheduler created and ENABLED.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate B: data/API verification pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: US and VN rows inserted for 2026-04-20..04-24. Idempotent rerun preserved row count at 2.

gates_result_snapshot.gate: Gate C: operations runbook completeness pass
gates_result_snapshot.notes: docs/gcp_cronjobs.md created with build/deploy/create/trigger/pause-resume commands. market_recap_operations.md updated with production deployment and rollback sections.

next_phase_considerations: Phase 8 (corpus staging) and Phase 9 (LLM semantic validation) are independent of the deployed job; no scheduler changes needed for those phases.
If adding a VN daily scheduler in future, use same europe-west1 location and a separate job name (e.g. daily-market-recap-vn-scheduler).
Monitor first few Saturday runs via Cloud Logging: jsonPayload.event='recap.run.outcome' AND jsonPayload.fields.inserted=true.

phases.title: LLM-based semantic validation for recap content quality
phases.objective: Replace brittle keyword-based content checks with deterministic LLM semantic validation for recap narrative quality, applicable to both Vietnamese and English outputs while preserving existing trust-critical deterministic checks.
scope_includes: Add a new semantic validator module that evaluates recap summary/bullets for required content dimensions (index context, macro context, and money-flow context) using a pinned model and strict JSON schema output.
Support multilingual validation prompts and scoring for at least VN and US/English recaps.
Keep trust-critical deterministic checks unchanged in validator.py (source existence, date window, allowlist checks, VN allowlist floor).
Add config/feature flag path to enable semantic validation per market/cadence without breaking existing runs.
Capture semantic validator evidence in structured logs (pass/fail, reasons, confidence, and short evidence quotes).
Add tests for semantic validator success/failure/invalid-JSON fallback behavior and validator integration coverage for VN and US.
scope_excludes: No changes to retrieval provider routing (Brave/Tavily selection remains as-is).
No public API schema change for recaps.
No additional scheduler/deployment work beyond phase 7.

implementation_checks: Semantic validation pass/fail output is deterministic in shape and parseable from strict JSON schema.
VN and US recap content can be validated semantically without relying on language-specific keyword literals.
When semantic validation fails or model output is invalid, the pipeline emits actionable failure reasons and does not silently pass.
Existing deterministic trust checks remain unchanged and continue to gate insertion.
Structured logs include semantic validation outcome fields for run diagnostics.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run semantic validator unit tests with mocked model responses for pass/fail/invalid-output cases.
Run validator integration tests for VN and US payloads that previously depended on keyword matching.
Run orchestrator/observability tests to verify semantic validation fields are logged and failures block insertion.
Run baseline lint and health checks.
required_evidence: semantic pass/fail fixture matrix for VN and US summaries
invalid-JSON model output fallback test evidence
structured log sample containing semantic validation fields
proof deterministic trust checks still gate hard-fail conditions
phase_gates: Gate A: semantic validator unit tests pass
Gate B: validator integration for VN/US semantic checks pass
Gate C: observability logs semantic fields correctly
Gate D: baseline lint/health pass

phases.title: Optional fetched-corpus staging persistence (pre-generation) with TTL
phases.objective: Persist fetched/parsed corpus before LLM generation for short-term replay and auditability, while avoiding permanent storage bloat and legal risk.
scope_includes: Add staging store for fetched corpus payload before LLM generation step.
Persist per-source extracted text + metadata for one run_id/week context.
Attach retention policy with strict TTL (recommended 7-14 days).
Add cleanup mechanism (scheduled purge or DB TTL job).
Wire generator to read staged corpus by run_id when enabled.
Add feature flag to keep default v1 behavior available (ephemeral-only mode).
scope_excludes: No change to public API payload.
No long-term permanent raw full-text retention.

implementation_checks: Corpus staging write succeeds before generation when feature flag is enabled.
Generator can consume staged corpus by run_id without behavior regression.
TTL purge reliably removes expired staged payloads.
Feature flag off preserves existing ephemeral flow.

agent_self_validation_steps: Run one recap with staging enabled and verify staged payload exists.
Run generation replay from staged payload (without re-fetch) in local test mode.
Run TTL purge job simulation and verify expired rows are deleted.
Run with staging disabled and verify default path unchanged.
required_evidence: DB evidence: staged rows inserted with run_id and expires_at
DB evidence: staged rows deleted after TTL purge
test evidence: replay from staging path passes
test evidence: feature flag off path unchanged
phase_gates: Gate A: staging persistence tests pass
Gate B: TTL cleanup tests pass
Gate C: backward compatibility pass
Gate D: baseline lint/health pass
